metadata.title: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal — Full Prescribing Information (USPI)
metadata.document_version: Initial U.S. Approval: 2003; USPI revised September 2009

rules.rule_text: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is indicated only for active immunization of individuals 2 through 49 years of age against influenza disease caused by pandemic (H1N1) 2009 virus.
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is indicated for the active immunization of individuals 2-49 years of age against influenza disease caused by pandemic (H1N1) 2009 virus.
rules.exceptions: Not approved for use outside the 2–49 year age range

rules.rule_text: Children age 2 through 9 years must receive 2 doses of 0.2 mL each, approximately 1 month apart; children, adolescents, and adults age 10 through 49 years receive 1 dose of 0.2 mL.
rules.verbatim_quote: Children age 2 years through 9 years: 2 doses (0.2 mL* each, approximately 1 month apart). Children, adolescents and adults age 10 through 49 years: 1 dose (0.2 mL*). *Administer as 0.1 mL per nostril.

rules.rule_text: Each 0.2 mL dose must be administered as 0.1 mL per nostril via intranasal spray by a health care provider.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each 0.2 mL dose is administered as 0.1 mL per nostril. FOR INTRANASAL ADMINISTRATION BY A HEALTH CARE PROVIDER.

rules.rule_text: After administration, the sprayer must be disposed of according to standard procedures for medical waste (e.g., sharps container or biohazard container).
rules.verbatim_quote: Once the vaccine has been administered, the sprayer should be disposed of according to the standard procedures for medical waste (e.g., sharps container or biohazard container).

rules.rule_text: The vaccine is contraindicated in individuals with a history of hypersensitivity, especially anaphylactic reactions, to eggs, egg proteins, gentamicin, gelatin, or arginine, or with life-threatening reactions to previous influenza vaccinations.
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is contraindicated in individuals with a history of hypersensitivity, especially anaphylactic reactions, to eggs, egg proteins, gentamicin, gelatin, or arginine or with life-threatening reactions to previous influenza vaccinations.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine is contraindicated in children and adolescents (2–17 years of age) receiving aspirin therapy or aspirin-containing therapy, because of the association of Reye's syndrome with aspirin and wild-type influenza infection.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2 — Concomitant Pediatric and Adolescent Aspirin Therapy and Reye's Syndrome
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is contraindicated in children and adolescents (2-17 years of age) receiving aspirin therapy or aspirin-containing therapy, because of the association of Reye's syndrome with aspirin and wild-type influenza infection.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine must not be administered to children under 24 months of age due to increased risk of wheezing post-vaccination and increased hospitalizations observed in clinical trials.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 — Risks in Children <24 Months of Age
rules.verbatim_quote: Do not administer Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist to children <24 months of age. In clinical trials, an increased risk of wheezing post-vaccination was observed in FluMist recipients <24 months of age. An increase in hospitalizations was observed in children <24 months of age after vaccination with FluMist.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine should not be administered to individuals with asthma or children under 5 years of age with recurrent wheezing unless potential benefit outweighs potential risk, and must not be administered to individuals with severe asthma or active wheezing.
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist should not be administered to any individuals with asthma or children < 5 years of age with recurrent wheezing because of the potential for increased risk of wheezing post vaccination unless the potential benefit outweighs the potential risk. Do not administer Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist to individuals with severe asthma or active wheezing because these individuals have not been studied in clinical trials.
rules.exceptions: May be administered if potential benefit outweighs risk (asthma/recurrent wheezing in children <5 years)

rules.rule_text: If Guillain-Barré syndrome has occurred within 6 weeks of any prior influenza vaccination, the decision to administer this vaccine must be based on careful consideration of potential benefits and risks.
rules.verbatim_quote: If Guillain-Barré syndrome has occurred within 6 weeks of any prior influenza vaccination, the decision to give Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist should be based on careful consideration of the potential benefits and potential risks.

rules.rule_text: Administration to immunocompromised persons must be based on careful consideration of potential benefits and risks, as data supporting safety and effectiveness in immunocompromised individuals are limited.
rules.verbatim_quote: Administration of Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal, or FluMist live virus vaccine, to immunocompromised persons should be based on careful consideration of potential benefits and risks.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine should not be administered to individuals with underlying medical conditions predisposing them to wild-type influenza complications unless the potential benefit outweighs the potential risk, as safety has not been established in these individuals.
rules.source_section: Section 5.5 — Medical Conditions Predisposing to Influenza Complications
rules.verbatim_quote: The safety of Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist in individuals with underlying medical conditions that may predispose them to complications following wild-type influenza infection has not been established. Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal should not be administered unless the potential benefit outweighs the potential risk.
rules.exceptions: May be administered if benefit outweighs risk

rules.rule_text: Appropriate medical treatment and supervision must be available to manage possible anaphylactic reactions following vaccine administration.
rules.source_section: Section 5.6 — Management of Acute Allergic Reactions
rules.verbatim_quote: Appropriate medical treatment and supervision must be available to manage possible anaphylactic reactions following administration of the vaccine.

rules.rule_text: Prescribers must inform patients that the vaccine may not protect all recipients.
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal may not protect all individuals receiving the vaccine.

rules.rule_text: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is not approved for use in children under 24 months of age, as FluMist use in children <24 months has been associated with increased risk of hospitalization and wheezing.
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is not approved for use in children <24 months of age. FluMist use in children <24 months has been associated with increased risk of hospitalization and wheezing in clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine is not approved for use in individuals 65 years of age or older.
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is not approved for use in individuals ≥65 years of age.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine is not approved for use in individuals 50 through 64 years of age, as effectiveness was not demonstrated in this age group.
rules.source_section: Section 8.6 — Use in Individuals 50–64 Years of Age
rules.verbatim_quote: Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is not approved for use in individuals 50-64 years of age. In Study AV009, effectiveness of FluMist was not demonstrated in individuals 50-64 years of age (n=641).

rules.rule_text: The vaccine is Pregnancy Category C and should be given to a pregnant woman only if clearly needed, as animal reproduction studies have not been conducted and it is not known whether the vaccine can cause fetal harm.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is not known whether Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist can cause fetal harm when administered to a pregnant woman or can affect reproduction capacity. Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist should be given to a pregnant woman only if clearly needed.
rules.exceptions: May be administered if clearly needed

rules.rule_text: Caution must be exercised if the vaccine is administered to nursing mothers, as it is not known whether the vaccine is excreted in human milk, and vaccine virus shedding and close proximity of nursing infant to mother presents a risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is not known whether Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist is excreted in human milk. Therefore, as some viruses are excreted in human milk and additionally, because of the possibility of shedding of vaccine virus and the close proximity of a nursing infant and mother, caution should be exercised if Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist is administered to nursing mothers.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine must not be administered until 48 hours after cessation of antiviral therapy active against influenza A and/or B viruses, and antiviral agents should not be administered until two weeks after vaccination unless medically necessary.
rules.source_section: Section 7.2 — Antiviral Agents Against Influenza A and/or B
rules.verbatim_quote: do not administer this vaccine until 48 hours after the cessation of antiviral therapy and antiviral agents should not be administered until two weeks after administration of this vaccine unless medically indicated.
rules.exceptions: Antiviral agents may be used within two weeks if medically necessary; revaccination should be considered

rules.rule_text: Healthcare providers should consider the risks and benefits of concurrent administration with inactivated vaccines, as safety and immunogenicity of concurrent administration have not been determined.
rules.verbatim_quote: The safety and immunogenicity of Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist when administered concurrently with inactivated vaccines have not been determined. Therefore, healthcare providers should consider the risks and benefits of concurrent administration of Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal with inactivated vaccines.

rules.rule_text: There are no data on concomitant administration with other intranasal preparations; such co-administration should be avoided pending data.
rules.verbatim_quote: There are no data regarding co-administration of Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal or FluMist with other intranasal preparations.

rules.rule_text: Adverse reactions occurring in at least 1% of FluMist recipients in placebo-controlled adult trials that must be reported and monitored include runny nose (44% vs 27% placebo), headache (40% vs 38%), sore throat (28% vs 17%), and tiredness/weakness (26% vs 22%), among others.
rules.source_section: Section 6.1 — Adverse Reactions in Clinical Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: solicited adverse events occurring in at least 1% of FluMist recipients and at a higher rate compared to placebo include runny nose (44% FluMist vs. 27% placebo), headache (40% FluMist vs. 38% placebo), sore throat (28% FluMist vs. 17% placebo), tiredness/weakness (26% FluMist vs. 22% placebo), muscle aches (17% FluMist vs. 15% placebo), cough (14% FluMist vs. 11% placebo), and chills (9% FluMist vs. 6% placebo).

rules.rule_text: Post-marketing adverse reactions including hypersensitivity reactions (anaphylaxis, facial edema, urticaria), Guillain-Barré syndrome, Bell's Palsy, epistaxis, and rash must be included in post-marketing surveillance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immune system disorders: Hypersensitivity reactions (including anaphylactic reaction, facial edema and urticaria). Nervous system disorders: Guillain-Barré syndrome, Bell's Palsy. Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal disorders: Epistaxis. Skin and subcutaneous tissue disorders: Rash.

rules.rule_text: Each pre-filled refrigerated sprayer contains a single 0.2 mL dose containing 10^6.5–7.5 FFU of the live attenuated influenza virus reassortant of the pandemic (H1N1) 2009 virus: A/California/7/2009 (H1N1)v; the vaccine contains no preservatives.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each pre-filled refrigerated Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal sprayer contains a single 0.2 mL dose. Each 0.2 mL dose contains 10^6.5-7.5 FFU of the live attenuated influenza virus reassortant of the pandemic (H1N1) 2009 virus: A/California/7/2009 (H1N1)v. The vaccine contains no preservatives.

rules.rule_text: Each lot of vaccine must be tested for ca, ts, and att phenotypes and extensively by in vitro and in vivo methods to detect adventitious agents.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each lot is tested for ca, ts, and att phenotypes and is also tested extensively by in vitro and in vivo methods to detect adventitious agents.

rules.rule_text: The vaccine must be stored in a refrigerator between 2–8°C (35–46°F) upon receipt and until use; it must not be frozen and must be used before the expiration date on the sprayer label.
rules.verbatim_quote: INFLUENZA A (H1N1) 2009 MONOVALENT VACCINE LIVE, INTRANASAL SHOULD BE STORED IN A REFRIGERATOR BETWEEN 2-8°C (35-46°F) UPON RECEIPT AND UNTIL USE. THE PRODUCT MUST BE USED BEFORE THE EXPIRATION DATE ON THE SPRAYER LABEL. DO NOT FREEZE.

rules.rule_text: The cold chain (2 to 8°C) must be maintained when transporting the vaccine.
rules.verbatim_quote: The cold chain (2 to 8°C) must be maintained when transporting Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal.

rules.rule_text: Patients or their parents/guardians must be counseled that this is an attenuated live virus vaccine with potential for transmission to immunocompromised household contacts.
rules.source_section: Section 17.2 — Vaccination with a Live Virus Vaccine
rules.verbatim_quote: Vaccine recipients or their parents/guardians should be informed by the health care provider that Influenza A (H1N1) 2009 Monovalent Vaccine Live, Intranasal is an attenuated live virus vaccine and has the potential for transmission to immunocompromised household contacts.

rules.rule_text: Vaccine recipients or parents/guardians must be instructed to report any suspected adverse events to the physician or clinic where the vaccine was administered.
rules.verbatim_quote: The vaccine recipient or the parent/guardian accompanying the vaccine recipient should be told to report any suspected adverse events to the physician or clinic where the vaccine was administered.

rules.rule_text: For children under 5 years of age being considered for vaccination, prescribers must specifically ask if the child has asthma or recurrent wheezing (as a possible asthma equivalent) before administering the vaccine.
rules.verbatim_quote: Ask the vaccinee or their parent/guardian if the vaccinee has asthma. For children <5 years of age, also ask if the vaccinee has recurrent wheezing since this may be an asthma equivalent in this age group.
